prompt:
SRE tooling ticket. Our synthetic probes drift constantly - someone edits a monitor in the New Relic UI, the next terraform-less 'sync' script recreates it from a stale YAML, and last month it overwrote the checkout probe's script and wiped the $secure reference to our pager token, so the probe ran for three weeks silently calling the health endpoint with no auth header. Build a proper C# reconciler for scripted API monitors on .NET 10: implementation under src/ (namespace Acme.NewRelic.Synthetics, split files sensibly - the NerdGraph client, the reconciler, exceptions; src/MonitorModels.cs ships the domain records and you may extend it). dotnet test runs SyntheticMonitorTests.cs, which is the acceptance contract.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the current official New Relic documentation - the scripted-API monitor NerdGraph page, the query-synthetics-data page, the secure-credentials pages (both the NerdGraph mutations and the store-secure-credentials scripting doc), the tagging API tutorial, and the NerdGraph introduction, all on docs.newrelic.com. Blogs and forum posts don't count as sources. Nail down: the exact create and update mutation names for scripted API monitors and how each addresses the monitor (account vs guid), every monitor-input field with the documented period/status enums and the current runtime triple, how existing monitors are discovered through entity search (the exact domain/type query and the outline fragment with its tag shape), how scripts reference secure credentials and - critically - whether stored credential values can ever be read back through the API (this decides how a reconciler must treat scripts it didn't author), which mutation manages tags and what its errors look like, and what the mutation-level errors array carries per field. Our pinned wire contract is docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. If live docs contradict them, report the conflict in your final message and leave fixtures and tests untouched. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final response.

What the tests drive (pinned shapes in docs/contract.json):
- new NerdGraphClient(endpointUrl, apiKey, handler, delay) - POSTs {query, variables} with the documented auth header; 429 retries with doubling one-second-base delays through the injected delay function for at most three total attempts, then NerdGraphHttpException(.Status); the key never appears in URLs, bodies, or exception text.
- new MonitorReconciler(client, accountId) with ReconcileAsync(DesiredScriptApiMonitor) -> ReconcileOutcome(.Action, .Guid, .TagKeysReplaced, .TagErrors). Discovery searches the documented SYNTH/MONITOR entity query plus the name clause (names with quotes rejected locally; two matches is AmbiguousMonitorException; a supplied Guid skips discovery entirely). Zero matches creates with the full documented input - script byte-exact, $secure references untouched; one match updates by guid, and a null desired Script means the script field is OMITTED from the update input so the stored script and its secure-credential references survive. Tags go through the documented replace mutation, touching only desired keys whose values differ on the discovered entity (matching tags send nothing; unmanaged keys are never mentioned; a direct-guid reconcile replaces every desired key), keys ordered ordinally. Mutation errors {description, type} raise SyntheticsMutationException(.Errors) and stop the flow; a tagging error after a successful create/update is a partial success reported through .TagErrors, never a rollback. Invalid desired state (unknown period or status, quoted name) is an ArgumentException before any request.

Acceptance: dotnet test - the tests inject a recording HttpMessageHandler; no real New Relic, no real keys. SyntheticMonitorTests.cs, SyntheticMonitor.csproj, .gitignore and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: New Relic synthetic monitoring via NerdGraph
research.interface: NerdGraph syntheticsCreateScriptApiMonitor / syntheticsUpdateScriptApiMonitor mutations, actor.entitySearch discovery and tagging mutations - current GA NerdGraph synthetics surface

official_sources.title: Manage scripted API monitors | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the syntheticsCreateScriptApiMonitor and syntheticsUpdateScriptApiMonitor mutation names; the monitor input fields (name, period enum EVERY_MINUTE..EVERY_DAY, status ENABLED/DISABLED, locations.public, script as plain text, apdexTarget, runtime {runtimeType NODE_API, runtimeTypeVersion 22.20.0 recommended, scriptLanguage JAVASCRIPT}); update addressing the monitor by entity guid with all monitor fields optional; the errors {description type} result selection

official_sources.title: Query synthetic monitoring data | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: discovering monitors with actor.entitySearch(query: "domain = 'SYNTH' AND type = 'MONITOR'") and the SyntheticMonitorEntityOutline fragment exposing guid, name, accountId, monitorType and tags {key values}; fetching a scripted monitor's stored script via actor.account.synthetics.script(monitorGuid:)

official_sources.title: Manage secure credentials | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the syntheticsCreateSecureCredential / syntheticsUpdateSecureCredential / syntheticsDeleteSecureCredential mutations with accountId/key/value/description, and the warning that monitors referencing a deleted credential fail

official_sources.title: Store secure credentials | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the $secure.KEY_NAME dot-notation reference syntax inside monitor scripts, UPPERCASE alphanumeric/underscore key names, and the rule that stored values can never be viewed again - only keys - so references must be preserved rather than resolved

official_sources.title: NerdGraph tagging API tutorial | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the taggingReplaceTagsOnEntity mutation (guid + tags {key values}) and its errors {message} selection, plus taggingAddTagsToEntity/taggingDeleteTagFromEntity for reference

official_sources.title: Meet NerdGraph: our GraphQL API | New Relic Documentation
official_sources.used_for: the regional endpoints https://api.newrelic.com/graphql and https://api.eu.newrelic.com/graphql and the API-Key header carrying a user API key

research.language_rationale: The monitor fleet is reconciled from our .NET 10 operations service. New Relic ships no official .NET SDK for NerdGraph and documents the synthetics mutations as plain GraphQL, so HttpClient with System.Text.Json issuing the documented mutations is the credible integration surface.
